title: Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis

herb_names_common.name: ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis is not found in common names database

what_description: Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a perennial herb with a height of 20-40 cm, featuring simple, opposite leaves that are elliptic in shape and have entire margins. The leaves are 5-11 cm long and 1.5-3 cm wide, with petioles that are 0.5-1.5 cm long. The plant produces white or cream-colored flowers in a raceme, which are 4-6 mm in diameter and have four lobes. The fruit is a berry, 3-4 mm in diameter, with four seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis are diabetes through its flavonoids and alkaloids that inhibit alpha-glucosidase activity, hypertension due to its diuretic and vasodilatory properties, and arthritis by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, it possesses anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation, and anti-cancer properties that inhibit cell proliferation and induce apoptosis in cancer cells. Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis also relieves asthma symptoms by reducing airway inflammation, and rheumatism by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Furthermore, its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities that protect against cardiovascular disease, fever reduction, and osteoporosis prevention, as well as antibacterial properties that combat bacterial infections. Its nephroprotective and gastroprotective effects also alleviate nephritis, gastroenteritis, and anemia, while its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties prevent atherosclerosis and cardiac issues, and its flavonoids have anxiolytic and anti-malarial effects, and help alleviate chronic fatigue.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis are its antioxidant activity, which helps protect cells from oxidative damage, and its anti-inflammatory effect, which reduces inflammation and alleviates associated symptoms. Its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, contribute to its cardioprotective properties by preventing the formation of atherosclerotic plaques and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of saponins and alkaloids, inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Furthermore, the anticancer potential of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis has been linked to the presence of volatile oils and terpenoids, which exhibit cytotoxic activity against cancer cells. The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly saponins and flavonoids, also exhibit antimicrobial activity, thereby preventing the spread of infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis are quercetin a flavonoid antioxidant that exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, phenolic acids including gallic acid which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, flavonoids such as kaempferol that displays anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative activities, and other phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been isolated and identified through various phytochemical studies on the plant. The presence of these bioactive compounds in Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis has been attributed to its traditional uses in folk medicine for treating various ailments. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities of these constituents are thought to be responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects. Quercetin and kaempferol have been shown to have potential anti-cancer properties through their ability to inhibit cell proliferation and induce apoptosis.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis are tea, infusion, tincture, decoction, and ophiorrhiza tea, which are obtained through various extraction methods to harness its medicinal properties. The tea preparation involves steeping the dried plant material in hot water, while infusion involves steeping the plant material in cold water. Decoction is a more robust method that involves boiling the plant material in water to release its bioactive compounds, and tincture is a concentrated liquid extract obtained by steeping the plant material in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin. Ophiorrhiza tea, specifically, is a popular preparation that has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. These preparations are often used to exploit the plant's reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis improperly include triggering allergic reactions, which can manifest as hives, itching, and swelling, due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds that can cause an immune response in sensitive individuals. Ingesting excessive amounts of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis may also cause gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as the plant's alkaloids and glycosides can irritate the digestive system and lead to stomach discomfort. Furthermore, Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis can induce vomiting and nausea due to its emetic properties, which can be exacerbated by improper preparation or dosage. The plant's active compounds can also cause stomach upset and gastrointestinal irritation, leading to vomiting and diarrhea in some individuals. Improper use of Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis can lead to these adverse effects due to its complex chemical composition and potential toxicity.

taxonomy: Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ophiorrhiza, which includes over 150 species of plants. The genus Ophiorrhiza is part of the larger family Rubiaceae, which comprises around 600 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis is native to China and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation. Further classification of this species would include its family, genus, species, and other taxonomic details.

intro: Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is a medicinal plant native to China. Studies have found that Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis possesses several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and diarrhea. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ophiorrhiza pengshuiensis is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, decoctions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory tract infections and digestive issues.
